Description: Launch the interactive step-by-step draft setup wizard
Notes: Walks through format / rounds / timer / game mode interactively

Description: Quickly create a draft with inline options
Notes: Skips the wizard; all parameters have defaults

Description: Join an active draft as a player
Parameters: team_name (optional text), pool (A or B optional)
Notes: Can only join while draft is in SETUP status

Description: Cancel and delete the current draft (confirmation required)
Notes: Shows a confirmation button before permanently removing the draft and all picks

Description: Start the draft and begin the first pick turn
Notes: Requires at least 2 players joined; starts pick timer

Description: Pick a Pokemon on your turn
Parameters: pokemon (name, required), tera_type (optional — Normal/Fire/Water/…), is_tera_captain (optional bool)
Notes: Auto-skips if pick timer expires; validates against ban list

Description: Ban a Pokemon during the ban phase
Notes: Only valid during BAN_PHASE (Custom format)

Description: Place a bid during an auction draft
Notes: Auction format only; checked against remaining budget

Description: Show the current draft status and whose turn it is
Notes: Shows round / pick number / current player / timer

Description: Show the full draft board with all picks so far
Notes: Paginated embed of all picks by round

Description: Create a new draft league for this server
Parameters: name (text required), format (snake/auction/tiered/custom optional), season (int optional)
Notes: Saves league to Google Sheets; one league per server typical

Description: View the match schedule
Parameters: week (int optional), pool (A or B optional)
Notes: Reads from Google Sheets Schedule tab

Description: Report a match result and update ELO ratings
Notes: Records ELO changes; DMs both players with new ratings; saves to Sheets

Description: View a player's drafted team
Notes: Shows full roster embed with type coverage

Description: Register your team name and logo
Notes: Logo must be a valid image URL (Discord CDN or public link)

Description: Propose a Pokemon trade with another player
Parameters: opponent (@mention required), give (Pokemon name required), receive (Pokemon name required)
Notes: Creates a pending trade; opponent must /trade-accept to complete

Description: Accept a pending trade offer
Notes: Trade ID shown in the trade offer embed

Description: Decline a pending trade offer
Notes: Trade ID shown in the trade offer embed

Description: Import a Showdown team from a .txt file attachment
Parameters: format (autocomplete: Gen 9 Smogon/VGC/Draft League), team_file (attachment: .txt Showdown export)
Notes: Parses the export and shows a Confirm/Cancel embed before saving; format stored as separate team

Description: Export your team in Pokemon Showdown format
Notes: Returns a pasteable Showdown team export string

Description: Check if a Pokemon is legal in a specific game
Notes: Checks console legality tables and VGC legality flag

Description: Full team analysis — type coverage / weaknesses / roles / archetypes
Notes: Powered by AnalyticsService; includes archetype label and threat score

Description: Compare two teams head-to-head and show advantage score
Notes: Leave both blank to compare the two most recent draft participants

Description: Show league ELO standings and win/loss records
Notes: Sorted by ELO descending; pulled from EloService + Sheets

Description: Submit a Pokemon Showdown replay link for a match
Notes: Auto-parses winner / Pokemon used / turns; records match result

Description: Upload a capture card video of your match
Parameters: opponent (@mention required), video (file attachment required), game_id (optional text)
Notes: Uploads to Cloudflare R2 or Azure Blob; generates ffmpeg thumbnail if available; max 100MB

Description: Look up a Pokemon's stats / tier / abilities / console legality
Notes: Returns embed with base stats / Showdown tier / VGC legality / sprite

Description: Challenge the AI bot to a live practice battle on Pokemon Showdown
Parameters: showdown_name (your Showdown username required), format (autocomplete — defaults to Gen 9 Random Battle)
Notes: Bot (NCL-Colress) sends you a challenge on Showdown; posts result when done. Trained models: gen9randombattle, gen9monorandom, gen7randombattle, gen6randombattle

Description: Pull latest code from git + reload all cogs + re-sync commands
Notes: Runs git pull --ff-only, reloads every cog, then syncs slash commands to the server — one-stop bot update

Description: Sync slash commands with Discord — pushes new or updated commands
Parameters: scope (guild = instant / global = up to 1 hour — defaults to guild)
Notes: Use after adding or changing commands; guild sync is instant and affects this server only

Description: Force-skip a player's pick turn
Notes: Advances the pick order; cancels the current timer

Description: Pause the active draft — freezes the pick timer and stops auto-skips
Notes: Draft stays in PAUSED status until /admin-resume

Description: Resume a paused draft — restores pick order and timer
Notes: Restarts at the current player's turn

Description: Replace an existing pick with a different Pokemon
Parameters: user (@mention required), old_pokemon (name required), new_pokemon (name required)
Notes: Edits the pick in-memory; does not re-save to Sheets automatically

Description: Reset the entire draft (CANNOT BE UNDONE)
Notes: Wipes draft state from memory; does not roll back Sheets rows already written

Description: Train AI models for ALL formats sequentially
Parameters: timesteps (int — default 500000), skip_existing (bool — default True), server (localhost/showdown/browser — default localhost)
Notes: Trains every format in TRAINING_MAP one at a time; DMs a summary when all done. localhost requires local Node.js server; showdown/browser require two Showdown accounts

Description: Train the AI bot for a battle format
Parameters: format (autocomplete from TRAINING_MAP), timesteps (int — default 500000), force (bool — retrain even if model exists), server (localhost/showdown/browser — default localhost)
Notes: Runs train_all.py in background; DMs result when done. localhost requires local Node.js server; showdown/browser require two Showdown accounts

Description: Download trained AI models from the latest GitHub Release into data/ml/policy/
Parameters: format (autocomplete — blank = all formats), release (tag e.g. ml-models-r5 — blank = latest)
Notes: Fetches the newest ml-models-r* release automatically; specific tag overrides default

Description: Open the local Showdown server health page in your browser + report TCP status
Notes: Opens http://localhost:8000 in the host machine's default browser; also shows reachability status. Ephemeral reply.

Description: View standings from the Google Sheets Standings tab
Notes: Read-only view pulled directly from Sheets

Description: Add a new match to the Schedule tab in Google Sheets
Notes: Modal prompts for player 1 / player 2 / week / date

Description: Record a match result manually in the spreadsheet
Notes: Separate from /result — does not update ELO; for manual corrections

Description: Log a trade / drop / add to the Transactions tab
Notes: For keeper leagues or waiver wire transactions

Description: Add a rule entry to the Rules tab
Notes: Rules are stored per-league in the Sheets Rules tab

Description: View or edit the league Setup tab (name, format, season, timer, etc.)
Parameters: action (view or edit — defaults to view)
Notes: Edit mode opens a modal to change a specific setting key/value

Description: Update a player's team name / pool / logo in the spreadsheet
Notes: Useful for correcting registrations or pool assignments

Description: Sync all local Pokemon data to the Pokedex tab (slow — up to 1000 rows)
Notes: One-time seed operation; takes ~10s for all Gen 1-9 data

Description: Add a playoff match result to the Playoffs tab
Notes: Tracks bracket results separate from regular season

Description: Show all available bot commands grouped by category
Notes: Reads discord_commands.csv at call time — always reflects current command list
